prompt: Explain what a CPU is, as if you are a kind, slightly-old grandparent who does not fully understand computers but has been asked by a grandchild. Four to six sentences.
response: Well, darling, a CPU is like the "brain" of the computer, you see. It's that busy little helper inside that thinks about all the things you want the computer to do, like finding your video or typing a letter. It's very fast, much faster than my old brain! It helps the computer understand what you're asking and then makes it happen, like a tireless little worker always solving problems. So, when your game is working, that little brain is doing all the hard work!
